item.title: Air Force unit faces flu outbreak weeks after vaccine mandate dropped
item.description: Dozens of service members at Lackland Air Force Base in Texas have fallen ill with the flu in the weeks since Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth rescinded the vaccine mandate, sources familiar confirmed to CBS News.

item.title: How the Obama Presidential Center is unique among presidential libraries
item.description: Former presidents and world leaders gathered in Chicago on Thursday to celebrate the grand opening of the Obama Presidential Center. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: NYC celebrates Knicks NBA championship with ticker-tape parade
item.description: The New York Knicks went up New York City's famous Canyon of Heroes for a massive ticker-tape parade celebrating the 2026 NBA champions.

item.title: H. R. McMaster says preliminary Iran deal "cuts very significantly against U.S. interests"
item.description: President Trump's memorandum of understanding with Iran is being met with criticism from both Republicans and Democrats. H. R. McMaster, former national security adviser to Mr. Trump, joins with his perspective on the deal.

item.title: Facing bipartisan criticism of Iran deal, Trump lashes out at "fools"
item.description: Republican Senators Bill Cassidy, Thom Tillis, Ted Cruz and Tom Cotton have been critical of the 14-point memorandum of understanding signed on Wednesday.

item.title: Initial U.S.-Iran deal draws mixed reactions from Republicans
item.description: President Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran on Wednesday night. The reviews are in, and Senate Republicans were either critical or cautiously hopeful that something better could be achieved. CBS News' Weijia Jiang and Nikole Killion have the latest.

item.title: CDC issues $107 million for emergency Ebola response
item.description: The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention set aside more than $100 million to strengthen its response to the Ebola outbreak in East and Central Africa. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul reports on the emergency funding.

item.title: Lawmakers react to U.S.-Iran agreement: "Colossal strategic failure"
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz is officially back open for business, at least for now. It comes after President Trump put pen to paper, signing the agreement that starts a two-month clock of talks with Iran. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more on congressional reaction.

item.title: Trump administration plans aggressive expansion of denaturalization cases
item.description: A Justice Department official tells CBS News that the Trump administration plans to revoke the citizenship of more than 250 foreign-born citizens by the fall. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the details.

item.title: Obama Presidential Center grand opening ceremony wraps in Chicago
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center held a star-studded grand opening in Chicago on Thursday. CBS News Chicago's Irika Sargent joins with highlights of the ceremony.

item.title: Man charged after toddler ends up in crocodile enclosure at zoo
item.description: Police in eastern England said a man was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after a 3-year-old boy "ended up in the crocodile enclosure" at a zoo.

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel won't withdraw from Lebanon, flouting U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday said the Israeli military will not leave its security zone in southern Lebanon despite the Iran memorandum of understanding. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has the latest.

item.title: Obama Presidential Center opens in Chicago with star-studded celebrations
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony featured a lineup of tributes from his family and closest colleagues, and musical performances by Bruce Springsteen, The Roots and more.

item.title: First named storm of 2026 Atlantic hurricane season forms, drenching parts of Texas, Louisiana
item.description: Tropical Storm Arthur formed on Wednesday off the Gulf Coast of Texas and drenched parts of the state as it strengthened. Arthur also caused flooding in parts of Louisiana. Jason Allen has more.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: High interest rates are on pause again. This is the savings account you should avoid.
item.description: Not every savings account is favorable in today's high-rate climate. Here's one you should specifically avoid now.

item.title: Janeese Lewis George projected to win D.C. Democratic primary for mayor
item.description: Kenyan McDuffie said he called Janeese Lewis George "to congratulate her on her victory and wish her success as she prepares for the general election."

item.title: Gas prices fall below $4 a gallon for the first time in nearly 3 months
item.description: The national average for a gallon of regular gas in the U.S. drops to $3.99, the lowest since March 30.

item.title: Former first lady Michelle Obama praises her husband in presidential center dedication speech
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama spoke at the dedication ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago on Thursday, calling it "an honor" to be by her husband's side. Watch her full speech.

item.title: Michelle Obama's speech at center opening moves husband to tears
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama delivered a loving and heartfelt tribute to her husband at the Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony in Chicago Thursday.

item.title: GOP congressman, missing for months, set to return on June 30
item.description: Republican Rep. Tom Kean Jr. of New Jersey will return to Congress on June 30, his spokesperson said, after being away since March in an unexplained absence that has confounded Capitol Hill.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic ramps up in wake of U.S.-Iran deal signing
item.description: More tankers are transiting the Strait of Hormuz after 111 days of gridlock, but Iran and the U.S. still have much to discuss.

item.title: The Fed just paused interest rates again. Here are 3 affordable ways you can still borrow money.
item.description: The Fed held rates steady again this week, but a few borrowing options are still cheaper than you may expect.

item.title: Watch: Obama speaks at dedication of presidential center in Chicago
item.description: Former President Barack Obama's new presidential center in Chicago held its grand opening on Thursday. Speaking at the dedication ceremony, Obama said he hopes the center will affirm "how precious our democracy really is."

item.title: Supreme Court sides with Texas man who challenged case on drug use, gun ownership
item.description: The Supreme Court unanimously found that a Texas man's prosecution for having a firearm while he was an occasional, unlawful marijuana user was too broad an interpretation of the law and violated his Second Amendment. CBS News' Jan Crawford reports.

item.title: Americans say they're reading fewer books. Here's what they're doing instead.
item.description: Many people are spending more time on screens, but also doing more physical activities, a new CBS News poll finds.

item.title: Supreme Court sides with man who challenged law barring drug users from having guns
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday ruled in favor of a Texas man who challenged a federal law that bars certain drug users from having firearms.

item.title: Photos show celebrations at Obama Presidential Center opening ceremony in Chicago
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center, museum and library opens in Chicago with a star-studded grand opening ceremony and public watch party on Midway Plaisance.

item.title: U.S. plans expansion of denaturalization push, aiming for 250 cases by fall
item.description: The Trump administration expects to try to revoke the U.S. citizenship of more than 250 foreign-born citizens by October, a Justice Department official said.

item.title: Explore the Obama Presidential Center and Library opening in Chicago
item.description: After more than a decade since it was announced, the Obama Presidential Center and Library officially opens to the public in Chicago's Jackson Park on June 19, 2026.
